title: Nicolae Balint (2011), "În Mureş - 1946, Partidul Comunist din România preia puterea. "Mic  îndreptar"pentru  revoluţionarii de profesie"
author: Balint, Nicolae
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
În Mureş – 1946, Partidul Comunist din România preia puterea. „Mic îndreptar” pentru revoluţionarii de profesie 
 Abstract (EN) 
 This article presents in a smart, engaging and honnest way, the 1946 electoral cycle and power consolidation in Mureș County, to show how the Romanian Communist Party (PCR) operationalized a county-level takeover through the  Blocul Partidelor Democrate  (BPD) and the  Uniunea Populară Maghiară  (UPM), combining administrative pressure, coordinated propaganda, and  systematic falsification of returns . Building directly on Simion Costea’s foundational studies—documenting  secret party reports , the role of the  Frontul Plugarilor , and local institutional capture (Apulum 2000; Angustia 2000; Anuarul Arhivelor Mureșene 2004)—Balint's article traces mechanisms from cadre placement and “professional revolutionary” playbooks to polling-station manipulation, parallel tabulations, and post-facto validation through party-state channels. By aligning communist tactical guidance with county-level evidence, the study demonstrates how a  scripted repertoire  transformed electoral competition into a controlled transition, while exploiting the ethnic segmentation of the electorate and the two-list strategy (BPD/UPM). The article argues that Mureș in 1946 offers a  micro-model  of authoritarian learning: the PCR refined techniques tested locally, then scaled them nationally in the subsequent consolidation of the people’s democracy. 
 Framed by the competition with the “historical parties” (National Peasant Party and National Liberal Party) and by the county’s  multiethnic  configuration, the study distills a  “Mic îndreptar pentru revoluționarii de profesie” —a concise checklist of tactics (front organizations, proselytism in minority constituencies, control of logistics and observers, manipulation of returns, and targeted post-electoral purges) that were  replicable beyond Mureș . By consolidating Costea’s pioneering findings with fresh synthesis, the article  bridges microhistory and national historiography , demonstrates the evidentiary value of  self-reporting communist documentation , and clarifies how a county-level apparatus transformed electoral competition into  controlled outcomes  in late-1946 Romania. 
  Keywords:  Mureș County; 1946 elections; Romanian Communist Party (PCR);  Blocul Partidelor Democrate (BPD) ;  Uniunea Populară Maghiară (UPM) ;  Frontul Plugarilor ; electoral fraud; secret reports; authoritarian consolidation. 
   
 Foundational works cited/engaged  
 
 
 Costea, Simion. “Aspecte ale instaurării puterii comuniste în România. Rolul Frontului Plugarilor în judeţul Mureş (1944–1947) /  Aspects Concerning the Coming to Power of the Communist Rule in Romania. The Role of the Ploughmen’s Front in Mureș County (1944–1947) .”  Apulum. Istorie  37, no. 2 (2000): 275–282. ISSN 1013-428X. 
 
 
 Costea, Simion. “ Alegerile falsificate din 1946 în judeţul Mureş .”  Angustia. Istorie  5 (2000): 175–188.  https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16150368 . 
 
 
 Costea, Simion. “ Din istoria Târgu-Mureşului: aspecte ale evoluţiei sale politico-sociale, demografice şi economice în perioada 1944–1989 / History of Tîrgu-Mureș: Aspects of Its Socio-Political, Demographic, and Economic Evolution (1944–1989) .”  Anuarul Arhivelor Mureşene  3 (2004): 255–271.  https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16505138 .
